design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore 3D futuristic environments in real-time, encounter non-random enemies, engage in turn-based Active Time Battle combat with resource management (HP, NRG energy, Bouge meter), solve character-specific puzzles, progress narrative across multiple planets
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Turn-based resource management during combat (balancing three separate resource pools: HP, NRG, and Bouge) combined with non-random encounter design that rewards exploration caution
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lead a misfit party of characters across a cyberpunk-noir galaxy as a down-and-out private investigator solving mysteries that threaten universal fate; experience unconventional humor and emotional character arcs
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately blends JRPG mechanics (party-based turn combat inspired by Chrono Trigger/Final Fantasy) with Western adventure game design (puzzle-solving via character-unique skills, narrative-driven progression); refuses random encounters in favor of visible/ambush enemies; uses qualitative stat descriptors (Poor/Excellent) instead of numeric values, emphasizing narrative feel over mechanical transparency; incorporates minigames and meta-mechanics (in-game camera for screenshots) as diegetic story elements

difficulty.lives_system: Party-wide: all characters must remain conscious; if entire party faints, game ends and must reload from last save
difficulty.checkpointing: Manual save game system; players must load previous save upon total party wipe
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Moderate: players control party composition (up to 3 of multiple characters), equipment/item selection, and MysTech loadout; combat positioning available; no documented difficulty settings mentioned

progression.upgrade_system: Character level progression via experience points grants improved statistics; MysTech system allows crafting/configuration of magic spells by placing colored bugs into Elementor Host slots; equipment and shield cells provide defensive options; unlocks include story-gated BattleSkills requiring specific plot developments
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-driven gates: MysTech cannot be used until awakened after specific story event; certain character BattleSkills require plot developments; shuttle travel to other planets unlocked at narrative checkpoint; character-specific skills (lockpicking, etc.) available to different party members
replayability_hooks: Multiple party member roster allows different team compositions on replay
In-game photography system with quest objectives encourages environmental exploration
Minigames embedded in field maps (Ox, Bugaboo remakes) offer optional engagement
Non-random encounter design rewards cautious exploration patterns

world_building_techniques: Cyberpunk film-noir aesthetic established through visual environment design (futuristic cities, space vessels, slums of abandoned planet Anachronox)
Non-player character dialogue and interaction reveals setting details and character motivations
Cutscenes during shuttle travel between planets advance narrative and world scope
Theme of 'working through troubles of one's past' embedded in protagonist and party character arcs
Visual storytelling through character emotive animations and facial expressions (enabled by Quake II engine modifications)

faction_indicators: Party-based alliance system: unlikely group of friends with unique skills suggests factional cooperation
Mystery investigation narrative creates antagonistic force (unspecified threat to universe fate)
danger_signals: Non-random enemy encounters visible on field maps create spatial threat awareness
Ambush encounters signal danger zones within exploration
environmental_storytelling: Slums of Anachronox planet establish economic disparity and protagonist status
Multiple planet environments (futuristic cities, space vessels, outdoor areas) convey galaxy-scale setting and scope
Interactive objects and puzzle environments reflect character abilities (lockpicking, etc.)
